content.text:
All tests pass. The reproduction test was already passing because the previous Green phase generalized the implementation using the Game of Life rules (`neighbors === 3 || (neighbors === 2 && liveCells.has(cell))`), which naturally handles the reproduction rule. No code changes were needed.

Green Phase Complete (Degenerate):
- Implementation: No changes required - the existing implementation in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-03_04-23-10_game-of-life-prose_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts` already handles dead cell reproduction correctly via the rule `neighbors === 3` applied to all candidate cells (including dead neighbors of live cells).
- Result: All 7 active tests pass (1 todo remaining).
- Approach: This is a degenerate Green - the previous generalization via APP (Absolute Priority Premise) when handling overpopulation made this test pass without additional code. This is exactly what TDD's minimal generalization predicts and confirms the implementation is properly aligned with Conway's rules.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

Relevant files:
- `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-03_04-23-10_game-of-life-prose_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts`
